cards.back: A set of conditions that guarantee the convergence of a nonlinear programming problem to its optimal solution, involving Lagrange multipliers and complementary slackness
A point in a function where the function value is smaller than at any other nearby point
The lowest value of a function among all possible input values
A point in a function where the derivative is zero or undefined, which can correspond to a local or global minimum, maximum, or saddle point
A necessary condition for optimality, where the gradient of the objective function is zero at the optimal solution
